jokes.text: You know, Wendy and I have been meeting with surrogates. She is so excited about my sperm being in another woman. It's weird.
jokes.setup: Mike casually mentions meeting with surrogates for their fertility issues
jokes.punchline: The awkward overshare about his wife's enthusiasm for his sperm in another woman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - inappropriate oversharing with oblivious tone. Strong character integration and uncomfortable specificity.

jokes.text: Why is my hair all spinning around and these things, like you, are doing nothin'?
jokes.setup: Selina at a wind farm photo op with her hair blowing around
jokes.punchline: She insults the wind turbines by comparing them unfavorably to her staff
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina - turning a practical problem into an excuse to insult everyone around her.

jokes.text: Is it too late to turn them into oil derricks?
jokes.setup: Clean energy photo op going poorly
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately wants to abandon the environmental message
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Selina's opportunistic nature and lack of principles.

jokes.text: Again?
jokes.setup: Being told her mother had a stroke
jokes.punchline: Treating a potentially fatal stroke as a minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - one word reveals Selina's callousness and suggests this is a pattern. Dark and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: She's been at death's door like five times
jokes.setup: Discussion of Selina's mother's health
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning she's a frequent near-death visitor
jokes.explanation: Solid setup for the grandmother's resilience, delivered with appropriate casualness.

jokes.text: It's just your toothy smile.
jokes.setup: Selina's mother supposedly comforting her after an election loss
jokes.punchline: The 'comfort' is actually a backhanded insult about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures toxic family dynamics - the fake comfort that's actually cruel criticism.

jokes.text: Looks weirder if you don't show your teeth.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Selina's smile being criticized
jokes.punchline: Practical observation about the no-win situation of smiling
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about the impossibility of pleasing everyone.

jokes.text: Oh, call Kent.
jokes.setup: Someone starts to share their mother's cancer story
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately redirects to business rather than engage emotionally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - completely avoiding emotional vulnerability by pivoting to work.

jokes.text: Wait a second, that's the O'Brien nimrod from the recount table.
jokes.setup: Seeing a protest on TV
jokes.punchline: Recognizing it as fake because they know the protester personally
jokes.explanation: Good political insider humor about recognizing manufactured outrage.

jokes.text: A fake protest. That is classic O'Brien. It's such a scumbag move.
jokes.setup: Identifying the protest as fake
jokes.punchline: The moral outrage at fake protests while presumably doing the same thing
jokes.explanation: Standard political hypocrisy humor - condemning tactics you also use.

jokes.text: Yep, I'll raid petty cash.
jokes.setup: Need to organize their own counter-protest
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to funding fake grassroots activity
jokes.explanation: Great economy - reveals the casual corruption in a matter-of-fact way.

jokes.text: Amy, I thought you were talking about the recount. Don't ever, ever scare me like that again.
jokes.setup: Amy calls with condolences about Selina's mother
jokes.punchline: Selina is more concerned about political news than her dying mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's twisted priorities - politics trumps family tragedy.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, is there a kettle or clothing steamer in here?
jokes.setup: Being shown a temporary mobile office
jokes.punchline: Gary's immediate concern is about garment care in a hospital crisis room
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary priorities - vanity and presentation even in family crisis.

jokes.text: But I made sure he had nowhere to sit.
jokes.setup: Explaining that Andrew (ex-husband) is at the hospital
jokes.punchline: Sue's petty revenge through seating arrangements
jokes.explanation: Perfectly efficient Sue moment - professional competence with personal spite.

jokes.text: You brought a date. / I don't know her.
jokes.setup: Selina accusingly noting Andrew brought his girlfriend to the hospital
jokes.punchline: Andrew's pathetic denial of his own relationship
jokes.explanation: Good rhythm between accusation and weak denial, showing Andrew's cowardice.

jokes.text: She's a smart one. You can maybe pick up something from her.
jokes.setup: Introducing his girlfriend to his ex-wife at a family crisis
jokes.punchline: Suggesting his ex-wife could learn from his new girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Incredibly inappropriate timing and suggestion - pure Andrew obliviousness.

jokes.text: I just want to say my wife is obsessed with your spiced eggnog. We drink it year-round. / It makes a really yummy ice cream. / Shut up.
jokes.setup: Monica's lifestyle brand being praised at inappropriate time
jokes.punchline: The escalating enthusiasm followed by Selina's sharp shutdown
jokes.explanation: Good build-up of inappropriate enthusiasm followed by justified shutdown.

jokes.text: No, I definitely didn't give you that sweater. Not that color. No, I didn't.
jokes.setup: Catherine saying Monica ('Monnie') gave her a sweater
jokes.punchline: Monica's increasingly panicked clarification about the sweater quality
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of social anxiety - Monica protecting her brand reputation.

jokes.text: She calls me 'Monnie.' Sounds like 'mommy.'
jokes.setup: Confusion over who gave Catherine the sweater
jokes.punchline: Monica explaining the uncomfortable nickname similarity
jokes.explanation: Solid uncomfortable observation about the weird family dynamic.

jokes.text: I mean, the campaign's over. I don't have to pretend to like country music anymore.
jokes.setup: Request to turn off Tim McGraw music
jokes.punchline: Selina admitting her campaign persona was fake
jokes.explanation: Perfect political cynicism - dropping the act now that votes don't matter.

jokes.text: Mee-Maw's favorite song is whatever is playing in the background at Neiman Marcus.
jokes.setup: Catherine defending grandmother's music taste
jokes.punchline: Selina's cutting assessment of her mother's superficiality
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific insult that perfectly captures both characters - grandmother's materialism and Selina's cruelty.

jokes.text: Honey, if I wanted to talk to an unconscious person, I'd book myself on Charlie Rose.
jokes.setup: Catherine suggesting Selina talk to her comatose mother
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to appearing on a notoriously boring talk show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant topical reference that works on multiple levels - media savvy, cruel timing, perfect structure.

jokes.text: She always wore dishwashing gloves when she gave me my bath. Which is why I don't do dishes. I just now realized that.
jokes.setup: Discussing mother's manicure habits
jokes.punchline: Revealing psychological trauma from childhood and connecting it to current behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark and brilliant psychological insight delivered casually - reveals deep maternal rejection and Selina's self-awareness.

jokes.text: So she's not gonna bounce back?
jokes.setup: Doctor explains brain death is final
jokes.punchline: Selina's hopeful question about recovery from death
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor about death - treating it like a minor setback.

jokes.text: It was just a cold sore, honey. I don't want to pay 40 grand for a herpes baby.
jokes.setup: Mike interviewing surrogate mothers
jokes.punchline: Crude assessment of medical condition vs. cost
jokes.explanation: Typical Mike - inappropriate crude honesty about sensitive medical/financial decisions.

jokes.text: Yes... yes. I mean, a lot of people think that I'm Jewish, but that... no.
jokes.setup: Surrogate asks if they're Christian
jokes.punchline: Mike's awkward over-explanation and denial of Judaism
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment showing Mike's social discomfort and clumsy handling of religious topic.

jokes.text: Our Lady... / ...of the Holy Womb... / ...of the Holy Womb. / ...Womb of Jesus.
jokes.setup: Being asked what church they attend
jokes.punchline: Making up an absurd church name on the spot with awkward coordination
jokes.explanation: Great improvised absurdity with the repeated 'womb' escalation and awkward synchronization.

jokes.text: There's not really a plug.
jokes.setup: Selina asking about 'pulling the plug'
jokes.punchline: Doctor's literal correction of the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective literalism humor - deflating the dramatic metaphor.

jokes.text: Oh, 'Stephen Hawking' here is indisposed, so I'll just...
jokes.setup: Gary having trouble with the phone
jokes.punchline: Comparing Gary to Stephen Hawking when technology fails
jokes.explanation: Good reference humor playing on technology struggles, though slightly dated.

jokes.text: I just needed to get a break from the Hindu Grim Reaper out there.
jokes.setup: Stepping away from the doctor
jokes.punchline: Racist characterization of the Indian doctor delivering bad news
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's casual racism and dark humor, though relies on ethnic stereotyping.

jokes.text: How is half a percentage point in the polls supposed to sweeten that shit biscuit?
jokes.setup: Kent mentioning polling improvement from mother's illness
jokes.punchline: Selina's colorful dismissal of small polling gains vs. major life decision
jokes.explanation: Great Selina-ism with 'shit biscuit' and shows her weighing personal tragedy against political gain.

jokes.text: if you will, a 'death bump.'
jokes.setup: Discussing potential polling improvement from mother's death
jokes.punchline: Kent's clinical terminology for profiting from family death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Kent's emotionless political calculation of family tragedy. 'Death bump' is perfectly quotable.

jokes.text: Well, that is some wild and heavy stuff. / Indeed. / Yeah. I got some real soul-searching to do.
jokes.setup: Kent explaining the political benefits of mother's death
jokes.punchline: Selina pretending to be morally conflicted while clearly calculating
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's fake moral wrestling while actually being convinced.

jokes.text: I can turn around, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Selina wanting a quiet place without Mike's stupid face
jokes.punchline: Mike's literal solution to the insult
jokes.explanation: Good Mike moment - taking obvious insults literally and offering inadequate solutions.

jokes.text: They were in the card your wife sent me when my cat Fibonacci passed.
jokes.setup: Ben sharing comforting words about loss
jokes.punchline: Revealing the source was a cat sympathy card
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of solemn moment - 'Fibonacci' is a great nerdy cat name for Ben.

jokes.text: Jesus, lose the fucking badge, man.
jokes.setup: Organizing fake protesters
jokes.punchline: Someone too obviously official-looking for grassroots protest
jokes.explanation: Solid political satire about the artificiality of manufactured protests.

jokes.text: What is that? What's 'totes'? / Totes, like all. Count all the votes.
jokes.setup: Sign reading 'Count Totes the Votes'
jokes.punchline: Confusion over slang and the awkward explanation
jokes.explanation: Basic generation gap humor around trying to be trendy with protest signs.

jokes.text: 18 to 34-year-old women are gonna be distracted by that. The only thing that women 18-34 are gonna do when they see you on camera is file a restraining order.
jokes.setup: Warning about putting Jonah on camera around young women
jokes.punchline: Escalating from 'distracted' to 'restraining order'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation insult specifically targeting Jonah's creepy appearance.

jokes.text: What do you pray for? / You, you know...
jokes.setup: Gary asking what Selina prays for
jokes.punchline: Selina's awkward non-answer revealing her narcissism
jokes.explanation: Subtle character moment showing Gary's devotion and Selina's self-centeredness.

jokes.text: 'O Lord God, it's me, Selina.' O Lord God. It's me, Selina...
jokes.setup: Gary trying to help Selina pray
jokes.punchline: Prayer formatted like 'Are You There God? It's Me, Margaret' children's book
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference that works well with the situation. Gary's earnestness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Lord, let her daughter, Thy humble servant, be the first woman elected President of the United States.
jokes.setup: Praying for her dying mother
jokes.punchline: Turning the prayer into a request for personal political success
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Selina can't even pray for her dying mother without making it about herself.

jokes.text: No, I mean actually lift me up 'cause my heel is stuck in this thing.
jokes.setup: Gary saying 'Lift her up, Lord' in prayer
jokes.punchline: Selina needing literal physical help with her shoe
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from spiritual to physical, deflating the solemn prayer moment.

jokes.text: Sounds like you shoplifted a bunch of vibrators.
jokes.setup: Multiple phones buzzing with notifications
jokes.punchline: Crude comparison to sex toy theft
jokes.explanation: Funny absurd comparison with good specificity, though relies on crude humor.

jokes.text: Oh, my prayer worked, right?!
jokes.setup: Getting good news about Nevada recount right after mother dies
jokes.punchline: Selina crediting her selfish prayer for political success
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the selfish prayer - perfect timing with mother's death and political victory.

jokes.text: You pulled the plug without me? / It wasn't a plug. It was a ventilator tube that they just...
jokes.setup: Catherine upset about missing grandmother's death
jokes.punchline: Selina focusing on technical medical details instead of emotion
jokes.explanation: Good callback and character moment - Selina avoiding emotional responsibility with technical precision.

jokes.text: We got good news about Nevada. - Wait, what? - We got good news from Nevada. - Mom?
jokes.setup: Staff celebrating political victory
jokes.punchline: Catherine's horror at celebrating during death announcement
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between political celebration and personal grief - tone-deaf staff

jokes.text: Thomas fucking Kinkade couldn't paint Mee-Maw in a positive light.
jokes.setup: Catherine wanting positive stories about grandmother for eulogy
jokes.punchline: Reference to painter known for idealized, saccharine imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reference joke - Thomas Kinkade's schmaltzy art style makes perfect comparison for impossibility of making mother look good.

jokes.text: what it was like to be the only daughter of a pathological narcissist. I mean, all this woman did was criticize me or ignore me.
jokes.setup: Selina describing her mother's narcissism
jokes.punchline: The ironic lack of self-awareness about her own narcissistic behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Selina describing behavior she exhibits while completely lacking self-awareness.

jokes.text: Really? Protesters? This country's just lost its sense of decency. - Well, they're animals. - No, no. Heavens, no. - They're well-wishers.
jokes.setup: Assumption that crowd outside are protesters
jokes.punchline: Revelation that they're actually supporters showing sympathy
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing how automatically Selina assumes hostility. Decent structure.

jokes.text: Oh, my goodness. I feel like a bride. A sad bride because, of course, this is a day of grief.
jokes.setup: Crowds of well-wishers at the house
jokes.punchline: Selina enjoying the attention then remembering to perform grief
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - genuine joy at attention followed by performing appropriate grief.

jokes.text: Oh, my goodness. I feel like a bride. A sad bride because, of course, this is a day of grief.
jokes.setup: Selina receiving sympathy from well-wishers
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction to maintain appropriate grief performance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's inability to maintain appropriate emotional performance consistently.

jokes.text: Did Eleanor Roosevelt eat pussy? / Oh, Jesus. / Did she eat pussy or did she just fingerbang her way down Pennsylvania Avenue?
jokes.setup: Amy making crude political comparison
jokes.punchline: Doubling down with even cruder elaboration
jokes.explanation: Shock humor that fits Amy's crude character but relies heavily on vulgarity for impact.

jokes.text: Did she eat pussy or did she just fingerbang her way down Pennsylvania Avenue? Fuck my mouth. I'm doing it again.
jokes.setup: Amy apologizing for crude language
jokes.punchline: Immediately doing it again with even cruder elaboration
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the inappropriate language joke, though relies heavily on shock value.

jokes.text: Apparently our born-again surrogate is super against DNR, so when POTUS pulled the plug on MOTUS, she started having second thoughts.
jokes.setup: Religious surrogate having moral qualms
jokes.punchline: POTUS pulling plug on MOTUS (Mother of the US)
jokes.explanation: Clever acronym humor with MOTUS, good political satire about moral complexity.

jokes.text: Billy Joel is gonna sing.
jokes.setup: Selina insisting she'll be president and have a lovely inauguration
jokes.punchline: Specific, achievable dream performer for inauguration
jokes.explanation: Good specificity - Billy Joel is perfect middle-brow choice for Selina's aspirations.

jokes.text: You're gonna cancel this recount like Anne Frank's bat mitzvah.
jokes.setup: Demanding the recount be cancelled
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to cancelling Anne Frank's bat mitzvah
jokes.explanation: Dark and shocking comparison that's historically inappropriate but memorably specific.

jokes.text: though I think the DJ already spent the deposit.
jokes.setup: Agreeing to cancel recount like Anne Frank's bat mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Taking the metaphor literally with practical concerns
jokes.explanation: Great callback that extends the inappropriate metaphor with absurd practical details.

jokes.text: Maybe I'll get assassinated.
jokes.setup: Everything going wrong politically
jokes.punchline: Considering assassination as potential solution
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about political desperation - perfectly delivered as casual consideration.

jokes.text: So who's fisting the American economy while he's here?
jokes.setup: Charlie Baird attending the funeral
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor for economic manipulation
jokes.explanation: Crude but fitting Selina humor about financial sector figures.

jokes.text: How soon after meeting you did he ask you to invest in his latest venture? - 17 seconds. - Really? - I timed it.
jokes.setup: Selina predicting Andrew's business pitch behavior
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific timing confirming the prediction
jokes.explanation: Good character-based prediction with specific payoff. The 17 seconds detail and timing element add precision to the joke.

jokes.text: Some can't-lose scheme about the third largest hotel chain in Brazil. / 'Can't lose.' / Can't lose. / Got to see if I still have my wallet.
jokes.setup: Andrew's business pitch being described
jokes.punchline: Gary checking his wallet after hearing about the 'opportunity'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of suspicion about Andrew's business schemes.

jokes.text: When do you have the time, then, Mom? When? Because you don't like Tim McGraw, then nobody is supposed to like Tim McGraw. Catherine, you are forbidden from saying the words 'Tim McGraw' ever again.
jokes.setup: Catherine complaining about mother never having time
jokes.punchline: Selina's authoritarian ban on country singer's name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Selina's control issues - presidential decree about country music

jokes.text: Unbelievable. Mee-Maw was so right about you.
jokes.setup: Conflict over Tim McGraw music at funeral
jokes.punchline: Catherine siding with dead grandmother against her mother
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic moment - Catherine finally standing up using grandmother's authority.

jokes.text: They're into ass play? No, they have a gift for sensing prevailing political winds.
jokes.setup: Selina's misinterpretation of 'wet-fingered'
jokes.punchline: Ben's serious clarification of political metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with Selina's characteristic misinterpretation of innocent phrase as sexual.

jokes.text: They're wet-fingered. / They're into ass play?
jokes.setup: Ben saying Qataris are good at sensing political winds
jokes.punchline: Selina's sexual misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual misdirection humor fitting Selina's crude mind.

jokes.text: We lost the popular vote. / Oh, God.
jokes.setup: Bad news delivered during mother's eulogy
jokes.punchline: The devastating political news at the worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect tragic timing - the worst political news delivered at the most inappropriate moment.

jokes.text: My mother had a good piano.
jokes.setup: Beginning the eulogy after emotional devastation
jokes.punchline: Starting with the negative piano story she planned to tell
jokes.explanation: Great callback that becomes touching - turning the negative story into something positive through grief.

jokes.text: I have lost... so much.
jokes.setup: Emotional eulogy breakdown
jokes.punchline: Unclear if mourning mother or political losses
jokes.explanation: Perfect ambiguity - genuinely sad but also about her political situation. Great character complexity.

jokes.text: Catherine, play the Tim McGraw thing.
jokes.setup: Breaking down during eulogy
jokes.punchline: Finally giving in to Catherine's music request
jokes.explanation: Good resolution of the music conflict - Selina finally surrendering control in grief.

jokes.text: your mother decided to leave the bulk of her estate to Catherine. / The bulk?
jokes.setup: Discussing inheritance arrangements
jokes.punchline: Selina's shocked focus on being cut out financially
jokes.explanation: Good final twist - one more way the mother gets the last word against Selina.

jokes.text: I hated my father.
jokes.setup: Funeral ending, people offering condolences
jokes.punchline: Tom's random dark confession
jokes.explanation: Good non-sequitur ending - unexpected dark confession as emotional button.
